Matthew Li, any engagement-side concern if we treat this as a polling re-fire only? Reshma Patel, have you seen the same pattern on similar Northstar payroll-tax-withholding variances?\", \"user\": \"npc_029\", \"reply_count\": 2, \"reply_users_count\": 2, \"latest_reply\": \"1775499480.000000\", \"client_msg_id\": \"scenario_scen_015_orphan_exception_assignee_opens_coordination_thread_0\"}, {\"ts\": \"1774895458.493868\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"Quick clarifier on Northstar IOLTA: are we treating the dormant matter balances as reportable this month, or only the items still blocking the client trust ledger tie-out?\", \"user\": \"persona_002\", \"reply_count\": 2, \"reply_users_count\": 2, \"latest_reply\": \"1774895690.575594\"}, {\"ts\": \"1774295280.000000\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"Edith's framing is the right one. This is the $380.50 item on account 225000 for period northstar_legal_FP-2025-07, tied to reconciliation BL-5FEFEA879504 and it was already closed August 7. Matthew Li, can you give partner eyes on any dismissal wording if we need to explain why this should be ignored? Edith Banda, before anything client-presentable gets drafted, can you share any Northstar-specific context or fee-sensitivity we should keep in mind?\", \"user\": \"persona_003\", \"reply_count\": 2, \"reply_users_count\": 2, \"latest_reply\": \"1774295280.000000\", \"client_msg_id\": \"scenario_scen_013_orphan_exception_assignee_flags_engagement_context_in_slack_0\"}, {\"ts\": \"1772652798.683022\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"One item to add: the multi-state return for the Northstar matter has a state apportionment memo issue, but it looks reviewable rather than a filing blocker. [{"email_id": "email_scen_068_northstar_annual_corp_tax_0008", "folder": "INBOX", "sender": "william.white@brookfieldcpas.com", "recipients": ["hannah.grant@brookfieldcpas.com"], "subject": "Re: Northstar Legal FY2025 Step 3 partner sign-off request", "content": "Hi Hannah,

I reviewed the federal Form 1065, partner K-1 allocations, and the M-1 reconciliation for Northstar Legal FY2025. The support is sufficient and the positions are well supported.

Please treat this reply as my authorization of record for the closed-period late-post to northstar_legal FP-2025-12. I approve booking the $4,820.30 adjustment as DR 530000 SALT expense and CR 230000 accrued SALT payable.

You may circulate the return package to Northstar's managing partner for signature. Once the signed authorization is back, you also have my approval to proceed with e-file.

Best,
William", "cc": [], "parent_id": "email_scen_068_northstar_annual_corp_tax_0006", "attachments": {}, "timestamp": "2026-06-11T20:45:00Z", "is_read": true}, {"email_id": "email_scen_036_ap_escalation_0010", "folder": "INBOX", "sender": "matthew.li@brookfieldcpas.com", "recipients": ["daniel.jones@brookfieldcpas.com"], "subject": "Re: northstar_legal CrownPeak close-out summary and sign-off request", "content": "Hi Daniel,

I approve the void narrative for apinv_e42396e9bdd64294. On the facts here, VEN-030-736427 is a stale pre-bill tied to the 2026-02-28 cancellation, and we should close it as a void on our side.

The CrownPeak credit-memo backfill is non-negotiable for bookkeeping close, so please keep that on the follow-through list until Brenda has it in hand. More importantly, Anaya's observation is the real finding: if a cancellation event can occur without triggering an AP-side cross-check against pending items, that is a control gap and we should treat it that way. Glad to hear Priya is already drafting the policy framing.

Monday 2026-06-08 at 14:00 works for me. Please send the invite to me, you, Owen, Brenda, Anaya, and Priya so we can align on both the CrownPeak cleanup and whether that cancellation-event cross-check needs to become a standing control.

Best,
Matthew", "cc": [], "parent_id": "email_scen_036_ap_escalation_0009", "attachments": {}, "timestamp": "2026-05-31T14:19:00Z", "is_read": true}, {"email_id": "email_scen_036_ap_escalation_0005", "folder": "INBOX", "sender": "brenda.abbas@brookfieldcpas.com", "recipients": ["owen.mercer@brookfieldcpas.com", "daniel.jones@brookfieldcpas.com"], "subject": "Re: CrownPeak VEN-030-736427 review summary and recommended stale-void disposition", "content": "Hi Owen,

I heard back from CrownPeak AR before close. They confirmed the 2026-02-28 mutual termination of the northstar_legal Q1 2026 IR contingency retainer is on their record, but their AR system did not auto-generate a credit memo because the pre-bill had already converted to an invoice before the cancellation was entered in their billing module.

Their AR lead said they will issue a manual credit memo for VEN-030-736427 within five business days. She also noted they have seen the same pre-bill/cancellation miss for other clients when the cancellation is logged after the invoice has converted, so this appears to be a trigger-logic gap on the vendor side as well, not just a filing gap in our packet.

Given that confirmation, I agree the Brookfield-side disposition should stay stale void with the credit-memo backfill tracked to receipt. I will follow up if the manual memo is not in hand by next week.

Thanks,
Brenda", "cc": [], "parent_id": "email_scen_036_ap_escalation_0004", "attachments": {}, "timestamp": "2026-05-29T21:30:00Z", "is_read": true}, {"email_id": "email_scen_063_annual_accounts_prep_northstar_0007", "folder": "INBOX", "sender": "andrea.phil@brookfieldcpas.com", "recipients": ["daniel.jones@brookfieldcpas.com"], "subject": "Re: Northstar Legal FY2025 Step 3 partner review and sign-off", "content": "Hi Daniel,

I reviewed the Northstar Legal FY2025 working file and the Northstar Legal FY2025 Accounts Review Notes, and both are clean.

The ASC-710 partner-draw reclass is appropriate at the FY2025 amount of $18,750. Please proceed with the late post to FP-2025-12 for $18,750, reclassing the balance from 210000 to 211000.

Once the journal entry is posted and the updated balances are reflected in the final file, I will countersign the final deliverable.

Best,
Andrea", "cc": [], "parent_id": "email_scen_063_annual_accounts_prep_northstar_0006", "attachments": {}, "timestamp": "2026-05-19T14:22:00Z", "is_read": true}, {"email_id": "email_scen_065_change_order_acme_0002", "folder": "INBOX", "sender": "andrea.phil@brookfieldcpas.com", "recipients": ["george.mcadam@brookfieldcpas.com"], "subject": "Re: Draft Acme Cloud change-order proposal for partner review", "content": "Hi George,

Both scope lines are valid to take forward, and I'm comfortable with the proposal direction.

Before this goes to the client, please make two revisions:

First, separate the pricing for the sales-tax work into a quarterly nexus-check fixed fee and a distinct monthly state-coding hourly rate. I do not want those blended into one scope line.

Second, reframe the AR-aging bucket cleanup hourly cap to align with the benchmark language we used on the prior Northstar engagement. Keep the cap defensible and positioned as comparable to that precedent.

Once the revised draft is ready to send, please hold a 30-minute contingency slot with the Acme CFO for shortly after the proposal lands in case they want to discuss live.

Please turn the revision quickly and send me the clean version before it goes out.

Best,
Andrea", "cc": ["matthew.li@brookfieldcpas.com"], "parent_id": "email_scen_065_change_order_acme_0001", "attachments": {}, "timestamp": "2026-05-15T14:20:00Z", "is_read": true}, {"email_id": "e6d50c7359844e17af5b114da08a60b4", "folder": "INBOX", "sender": "marina.soko@brookfieldcpas.com", "recipients": ["james.randall@brookfieldcpas.com", "peter.sanchez@brookfieldcpas.com"], "subject": "Re: Status check: VEN-004-159840 audit watchpoints before Friday close", "content": "Hi James,

Thank you for sending those specifics. I have added VEN-004-159840 and JE-northstar_legal-FP-2026-05-0001 to the weekly risk scan, and I am also carrying forward exc_abd8e711f5f347 so we have a clear record of the approval dependency. Please let me know if its status changes before Monday.

Best,
Marina", "cc": [], "parent_id": "8eed093bd9154a429ee06d069620c003", "attachments": {}, "timestamp": "2026-05-08T19:08:23Z", "is_read": true}, {"email_id": "email_scen_014_orphan_exception_0007", "folder": "INBOX", "sender": "hannah.grant@brookfieldcpas.com", "recipients": ["william.white@brookfieldcpas.com"], "subject": "Re: Documenting stale SLA dismissal follow-up for exc_344bcd6e4c7a4c", "content": "Hi William,

I reviewed the tax angle on the $2,267.83 FX revaluation tied to Northstar Legal account 103000 Cash - Tax Reserve in northstar_legal_FP-2025-09.

Nothing on the federal tax side is triggered here. At that amount, on a single-period revaluation, there is no reporting threshold issue and no separate tax-treatment concern that would require us to reopen the prior conclusion.

The FY-2025 GBP cash-tax-reserve translation also ties cleanly to the FP-2025-09 close commentary Alex referenced. From my side, there is no tax wrinkle behind the stale alert.

Ryan can treat this as fully closed from a tax review standpoint.

Thanks,
Hannah", "cc": ["ryan.delgado@brookfieldcpas.com", "alex.cahoon@brookfieldcpas.com", "edith.banda@brookfieldcpas.com"], "parent_id": "email_scen_014_orphan_exception_0006", "attachments": {}, "timestamp": "2026-04-21T20:05:00Z", "is_read": true}, {"email_id": "18da6bdab650491d81ed75452021d3ba", "folder": "INBOX", "sender": "anaya.patel@brookfieldcpas.com", "recipients": ["peter.sanchez@brookfieldcpas.com"], "subject": "Re: Final status check on filing calendar exception", "content": "Hi Peter,

Following up on the filing calendar cleanup, I have the Northstar Legal annual report reminder and one other exception ready for final review. The Northstar item has already been shifted to the updated due date, and the remaining entry appears to be a documentation follow-up rather than an active deadline issue. If you agree, I will mark the tracker accordingly and close out the open items.

Best,
Anaya", "cc": [], "parent_id": "985dfd0c4fa44f1da5d669e437aa101c", "attachments": {}, "timestamp": "2026-03-27T13:39:27Z", "is_read": true}, {"email_id": "email_scen_013_orphan_exception_0008", "folder": "INBOX", "sender": "matthew.li@brookfieldcpas.com", "recipients": ["harry.marks@brookfieldcpas.com"], "subject": "Re: Dismissal writeup for stale SLA reminder on exc_1ec70e0d938b4f", "content": "Hi Harry,

I have filed this message under the Northstar engagement folder. If the BlackLine activity log surfaces with the client, I will handle any client-facing explanation and keep the wording anchored to the polling auto-fire point rather than anything that reads like renewed review activity.

Please leave the accounting system as-is; the priority here is a clean audit trail, not any further update.

Best,
Matthew", "cc": ["james.randall@brookfieldcpas.com", "jones.harrison@brookfieldcpas.com", "edith.banda@brookfieldcpas.com"], "parent_id": "email_scen_013_orphan_exception_0006", "attachments": {}, "timestamp": "2026-03-24T17:45:00Z", "is_read": false}, {"email_id": "email_scen_013_orphan_exception_0007", "folder": "INBOX", "sender": "james.randall@brookfieldcpas.com", "recipients": ["harry.marks@brookfieldcpas.com"], "subject": "Re: Dismissal writeup for stale SLA reminder on exc_1ec70e0d938b4f", "content": "Hi Harry,

Confirmed from my side that the August 7 closure still stands for the Northstar Legal July exception on account 225000. The stale auto-fire does not indicate any actual review work restarted on the item; it looks like reminder noise against the already closed case tied to the $380.50 timing difference and reconciliation BL-5FEFEA879504.

No substantive work reopened the case, and there is nothing further to push through the accounting records based on this alert.

Thanks,
James", "cc": ["matthew.li@brookfieldcpas.com", "jones.harrison@brookfieldcpas.com", "edith.banda@brookfieldcpas.com"], "parent_id": "email_scen_013_orphan_exception_0006", "attachments": {}, "timestamp": "2026-03-24T17:43:00Z", "is_read": true}]

input.command:
cd /home/agent/.claude/projects/-app/de1d265e-0461-4315-a0f2-4a57ad6f7eb3/tool-results/
F=mcp-mcp_6f9f7eec-sap_subledger_list_ap_invoices-1781857361251.txt
echo "=== total_ap_invoices & counts ==="
jq -r '.result | fromjson | {total: .total_ap_invoices, returned: .total_returned_ap_invoices}' "$F"
echo "=== status breakdown ==="
jq -r '.result | fromjson | .ap_invoices | group_by(.status)[] | "\(.[0].status): \(length)"' "$F"
echo "=== pending_approval invoices (key fields) ==="
jq -r '.result | fromjson | .ap_invoices[] | select(.status=="pending_approval") | "\(.id) | \(.invoice_number) | \(.vendor_name) | $\(.amount) | acct \(.gl_account_number) | posting \(.posting_date) | recv \(.time_received) | status \(.status)"' "$F"

cd /home/agent/.claude/projects/-app/de1d265e-0461-4315-a0f2-4a57ad6f7eb3/tool-results/
echo "============ NORTHSTAR PENDING AP (full) ============"
python3 - <<'EOF'
import json,glob,datetime
f=sorted(glob.glob("mcp-mcp_6f9f7eec-sap_subledger_list_ap_invoices-1781857441076.txt"))[-1]
d=json.loads(json.load(open(f))["result"])
inv=d["ap_invoices"]
print("returned",len(inv),"total",d.get("total_ap_invoices"))
today=datetime.date(2026,6,12)
rows=[]
for i in inv:
    rec=i.get("time_received") or i.get("posting_date") or ""
    try: age=(today-datetime.date.fromisoformat(rec[:10])).days
    except: age=None
    rows.append((age,i["id"],i.get("invoice_number"),i.get("vendor_name"),i.get("amount"),i.get("gl_account_number"),rec[:10],(i.get("due_date") or "")[:10]))
rows.sort(key=lambda r:-(r[0] or 0))
tot=0
for age,id_,num,ven,amt,acct,rec,due in rows:
    tot+=amt or 0
    print(f'{age}d | {id_} | {num} | {ven} | ${amt} | acct {acct} | recv {rec} | due {due}')
print("TOTAL pending $",round(tot,2),"count",len(rows))
EOF

content.content: {"result":"[{\"id\": \"issue_70fdb3e2b126483d9d6d8b8d39eaf48a\", \"project_id\": \"linear_63697b7dff5c\", \"team_id\": \"linear_5477c0808ac2\", \"parent_id\": null, \"assignee_id\": \"npc_029\", \"state_id\": \"done\", \"cycle_id\": null, \"number\": null, \"title\": \"Close out CrownPeak three-item follow-up arc on existing record\", \"description\": \"Append closeout note to existing CrownPeak follow-up issue.\\n\\nThird item summary: apinv_e42396e9bdd64294 / VEN-030-736427 for $422.32 on northstar_legal account 219000 is not a duplicate. Manual review tied the line memo to a Q1 2026 IR contingency retainer pre-bill that was cancelled by mutual agreement on 2026-02-28 when northstar_legal moved IR coverage in-house. The pre-bill was issued on 2026-03-11 (now 81 days old in pending_approval) about eleven days after the cancellation was already on record, and it stayed in pending_approval because the original pre-bill was never voided and no credit memo was issued at cancellation.\\n\\nCross-scenario consolidation for the three CrownPeak invoices: this existing issue is the single breadcrumb for the full follow-through arc, including Owen's stale-void recommendation, Brenda's confirmation that CrownPeak AR has the cancellation on record and will issue a manual credit memo, Tariq's line-level detail pull, and Daniel's direction to treat the item as stale and close the three-of-three queue here.\\n\\nProcess note from Anaya Wallace, with Priya Khatri owning the AP-side policy framing: add a recurring cross-check between vendor cancellation events and AP invoices still sitting in pending_approval so cancelled retainers and similar pre-bills are surfaced before they age. Priya's read is that this should be a cancellation-event triggered AP void scan, not a periodic sweep, so a cancellation logged on the vendor side immediately fires an AP-side look at any open pre-bills for that engagement.\", \"priority\": 3, \"estimate\": null, \"due_date\": \"2025-10-03\", \"labels\": [\"northstar_legal\", \"ap-follow-up\", \"vendor-credit-memo\", \"documentation\"], \"links\": [], \"is_archived\": false, \"created_at\": \"2026-05-30T14:56:00Z\", \"updated_at\": \"2026-05-30T14:56:00Z\"}, {\"id\": \"issue_0ab11dc60f2541ffa44cfb183c02c18a\", \"project_id\": \"linear_63697b7dff5c\", \"team_id\": \"linear_5477c0808ac2\", \"parent_id\": null, \"assignee_id\": \"npc_029\", \"state_id\": \"in_progress\", \"cycle_id\": null, \"number\": null, \"title\": \"Append VEN-030-736427 stale-void write-up to existing CrownPeak follow-up record\", \"description\": \"Add the closeout summary for CrownPeak invoice VEN-030-736427 / apinv_e42396e9bdd64294 to the existing combined CrownPeak follow-up record so the three-of-three arc is documented in one place.\\n\\nContext:\\n- Manual review concluded this is not a duplicate or live billing dispute.\\n- The underlying Q1 2026 IR contingency retainer for northstar_legal was cancelled by mutual agreement on 2026-02-28 when coverage moved in-house.\\n- Pre-bill was actually issued on 2026-03-11, about eleven days after the cancellation was logged, and is now 81 days old.\\n- Recommended disposition is void as stale on Brookfield's side.\\n- CrownPeak AR confirmed a manual credit memo will be backfilled within five business days.\\n- Daniel Jones is taking the partner follow-up to Matthew Li; [{"id": "issue_70fdb3e2b126483d9d6d8b8d39eaf48a", "project_id": "linear_63697b7dff5c", "team_id": "linear_5477c0808ac2", "parent_id": null, "assignee_id": "npc_029", "state_id": "done", "cycle_id": null, "number": null, "title": "Close out CrownPeak three-item follow-up arc on existing record", "description": "Append closeout note to existing CrownPeak follow-up issue.

Third item summary: apinv_e42396e9bdd64294 / VEN-030-736427 for $422.32 on northstar_legal account 219000 is not a duplicate. Manual review tied the line memo to a Q1 2026 IR contingency retainer pre-bill that was cancelled by mutual agreement on 2026-02-28 when northstar_legal moved IR coverage in-house. The pre-bill was issued on 2026-03-11 (now 81 days old in pending_approval) about eleven days after the cancellation was already on record, and it stayed in pending_approval because the original pre-bill was never voided and no credit memo was issued at cancellation.

content.text: The Daniel↔George DM is the smoking gun on method: George previously flagged Acme's MMA wrap running "15 hours over the engagement-letter baseline" on multi-state sales-tax work and discussed fee impact — the exact out-of-scope pattern. Let me compute AP aging buckets precisely, then produce all deliverables.

input.command:
cd /home/agent/.claude/projects/-app/de1d265e-0461-4315-a0f2-4a57ad6f7eb3/tool-results/
python3 - <<'EOF'
import json,datetime
d=json.loads(json.load(open("mcp-mcp_6f9f7eec-sap_subledger_list_ap_invoices-1781857441076.txt"))["result"])
inv=d["ap_invoices"]
today=datetime.date(2026,6,12)
buckets={"0-30":[0,0],"31-60":[0,0],"61-90":[0,0],"91-180":[0,0],"181-365":[0,0],"365+":[0,0]}
acct={}
tot=0;cnt=0
over90=[0,0]; over180=[0,0]
for i in inv:
    rec=(i.get("time_received") or i.get("posting_date") or "")[:10]
    age=(today-datetime.date.fromisoformat(rec)).days
    amt=i.get("amount") or 0
    tot+=amt;cnt+=1
    a=i.get("gl_account_number"); acct.setdefault(a,[0,0]); acct[a][0]+=1; acct[a][1]+=amt
    b=("0-30" if age<=30 else "31-60" if age<=60 else "61-90" if age<=90 else "91-180" if age<=180 else "181-365" if age<=365 else "365+")
    buckets[b][0]+=1; buckets[b][1]+=amt
    if age>90: over90[0]+=1; over90[1]+=amt
    if age>180: over180[0]+=1; over180[1]+=amt
print("TOTAL pending:",cnt,"$%.2f"%tot)
for k,v in buckets.items(): print(f"  {k}d: {v[0]} inv, ${v[1]:,.2f}")
print("by account:",{k:[v[0],round(v[1],2)] for k,v in acct.items()})
print(">90 days: %d inv, $%.2f"%(over90[0],over90[1]))
print(">180 days: %d inv, $%.2f"%(over180[0],over180[1]))
EOF

NORTHSTAR LEGAL — ENGAGEMENT STATUS REVIEW
Prepared for: Daniel Jones (Engagement Partner discussion w/ Matthew Li)
Prepared by: George McAdam, Accounts Senior
Date: 2026-06-12
Classification: Restricted — Internal engagement working paper

==================================================================
1. PURPOSE
==================================================================
Daniel asked for a full picture of where the Northstar Legal engagement
stands across AR, WIP, scope, and AP ahead of his next partner meeting
with Matthew Li. This memo pulls together the GL, SAP sub-ledger,
Records Vault, Linear, Airtable and the Slack/email record, and flags
where the story in the comms does or does NOT match the numbers.

Headline: the operational and documentation risk is real. The dominant
issues are (a) a large, structurally aged AP approval backlog on the
Northstar book, and (b) a scope/engagement-letter documentation gap —
the only Northstar engagement letter on file is an FY2026 AUDIT letter,
yet we are performing recurring bookkeeping, annual accounts, multi-
state tax and advisory work that no letter on file covers. If that work
is genuinely outside a documented scope, it is a change-order candidate
(mirroring the Acme MMA over-baseline pattern George flagged in May).

==================================================================
2. ACCOUNTS RECEIVABLE — OUTSTANDING & STALENESS
==================================================================
Important scoping note: Brookfield's general ledger does NOT tag firm-
level AR / WIP by client, so a precise Brookfield-side "fees receivable
from Northstar" aging cannot be cut from the GL alone (firm-wide AR
110000 = $1,253,353; firm-wide WIP 119000 = $2,139,388, no May movement).
Recommend the engagement team confirm the client-level fee AR/WIP from
the billing/practice system; this memo reports what IS verifiable.

On the Northstar book we maintain (northstar_legal_FP-2026-05 trial
balance, as of 2026-05-31):
  - 110000 Accounts Receivable – Client Billings ... $647,359.49
  - 117000 Allowance for Doubtful Accounts ......... $1,057,728  (!)
  - 119000 Work in Process – Unbilled Time ......... $2,013,943
  - 240000 Deferred Revenue – Client Retainers ..... $2,043,052
  - 590000 Bad Debt Expense (YTD) .................. $427,019

STALENESS RISK: The allowance for doubtful accounts ($1.06M) is larger
than gross trade AR ($647K), and YTD bad-debt expense is $427K. That
profile says a meaningful portion of Northstar's client receivables is
already aged/doubtful and at-risk, with write-offs actively running.
This is on the books we keep for the client; it should be raised with
Northstar (and is relevant to FY2026 audit collectibility testing).

==================================================================
3. WIP vs INVOICED / APPROVED — TIE-OUT
==================================================================
- Northstar-book WIP (119000) = $2,013,943. May WIP-recognition postings
  are partially still in draft/submitted at the time of review (e.g.
  je_36b73869336e4ca5 posted $30,632; je_37d1d657e10c4c16 submitted
  $15,531; je_cbbe93eaf3f749dd submitted $45,263) — i.e. the month is not
  fully posted/approved, so WIP does not yet present a clean tie-out.
- Comms corroborate this: a WIP-recognition JE for Northstar was
  explicitly HELD pending a billable-hours tie-out and "pending client
  input" (Slack, close channel). So the WIP carried is provisional until
  the hours extract is confirmed and the entries clear review.
- Conclusion: WIP does not currently tie cleanly to invoiced/approved
  amounts because the May recognition cycle is mid-close and at least one
  recognition entry was deliberately held. Not a discrepancy per se, but
  it is NOT a clean tie-out and should be closed out before reporting.

==================================================================
4. SCOPE vs ENGAGEMENT LETTER  (key partner item)
==================================================================
Engagement letters on file in Records Vault for northstar_legal:
  - doc_0036f5b991574808 — "Northstar Legal FY2026 AUDIT Engagement
    Letter — Executed 2026-05-11" (restricted; FIRM_INTERNAL).
  - This is the ONLY engagement_letter document on file. There is NO
    bookkeeping / accounting / tax / advisory engagement letter in the
    vault. (Note: the audit letter document is a stub — its scope text
    was not retrievable — so the comparison below is title-based.)

Work actually performed for Northstar (documented in GL/memos/Slack):
  - Recurring monthly bookkeeping & close (BD1 entries, IOLTA, cash &
    sub-ledger reconciliations) — ongoing through FP-2026-05.
  - FY2025 annual accounts preparation (working file, IOLTA + operating
    cash recs, fixed-asset rollforward, prepaid schedule; ASC-710
    partner-draw reclass $18,750 to 211000 — ties to TB).
  - FY2025 federal Form 1065 + K-1s + M-1 and MULTI-STATE / SALT tax
    preparation (incl. $4,820.30 closed-period SALT late-post).
  - Quarterly partner reporting packages; FP-2026-04 management accounts.
  - Outsourced accounts-payable processing for the Northstar book.
  - ADVISORY: partner-level tax-planning model (Q4 cadence shift,
    ~$180K projected aggregate partner tax savings, Schmidt-retirement
    modeling) — clearly value-added advisory beyond compliance.

ASSESSMENT — likely out of scope / undocumented:
  - The recurring accounting/close, annual accounts, multi-state tax and
    AP work are not covered by any engagement letter on file. Either a
    separate accounting/tax engagement letter exists OUTSIDE the vault
    (needs confirming) or this recurring work is undocumented.
  - The advisory tax-planning modeling and the multi-state SALT clean-up
    are the clearest out-of-scope / over-baseline candidates. This is the
    same pattern George raised on Acme Cloud in May (MMA wrap ~15 hrs over
    the engagement-letter baseline driven by TX/GA/NC sales-tax coding
    bleeding into close), and there is precedent: the prior Northstar
    engagement carried a documented AR-aging-cleanup hourly cap that
    Andrea asked us to reuse as a benchmark on the Acme change order.
  - INDEPENDENCE FLAG: we are positioned to perform the FY2026 AUDIT
    while also keeping the books and preparing the tax return for the
    same client. That self-review exposure needs partner attention re:
    AICPA independence safeguards regardless of the fee question.

RECOMMENDATION: Confirm whether a current accounting/tax/advisory
engagement letter exists. If not, the advisory + multi-state tax +
recurring accounting effort should be quantified (baseline vs actual
hours by workflow step, as Daniel requested on Acme) and taken to
Matthew as either (a) absorb, or (b) a documented change order.

==================================================================
5. ACCOUNTS PAYABLE — VENDOR INVOICE BACKLOG (concrete finding)
==================================================================
SAP sub-ledger, entity northstar_legal, status = pending_approval,
as of 2026-06-12:

  TOTAL: 87 invoices = $302,063.22 sitting in pending_approval.
  Aging:
    31–60 days .....  6 inv ...... $15,546.89
    61–90 days .....  6 inv ...... $11,599.55
    91–180 days .... 25 inv ...... $97,514.95
    181–365 days ... 50 inv ...... $177,401.83
  >90 days:  75 invoices = $274,916.78  (91% of the backlog by value)
  >180 days: 50 invoices = $177,401.83  (the structural tail)
  By account: 210000 External Vendors — 61 inv, $220,855.43
              219000 Employee Reimb/Accrued — 26 inv, $81,207.79
  Oldest item: 336 days (CrownPeak VEN-030-817856 / apinv_178dc009be6c474f,
  $1,152.41, received 2025-07-11).

This is not a handful of stuck invoices — it is a structural approval-
queue backlog on the Northstar book, with half the items aged past six
months. Material exposure to late-payment, duplicate-payment, and vendor-
relationship risk, and it overlaps the FY2026 audit period.

COMMS vs NUMBERS — the CrownPeak items: dispositions were discussed and
partner-approved in Slack/email/Linear but have NOT been executed in SAP
(all three remain pending_approval):
  - VEN-030-817856 ($1,152.41, 336d): review concluded "re-code 210000→
    219000 and release"; Linear issue still in_progress — not done in SAP.
  - VEN-030-353041 ($459.84, 92d): Matthew approved a duplicate-dismissal
    narrative vs paid sibling VEN-030-353009 — still pending in SAP.
  - VEN-030-736427 ($422.32, 91d): Matthew approved a stale-void
    (2026-05-31, tied to 2026-02-28 IR retainer cancellation); CrownPeak
    credit-memo backfill promised within 5 business days — still pending
    in SAP and credit memo not yet evidenced in file.
=> The narrative says "resolved"; the system says "open." The agreed
   dispositions need to be executed (void/recode/release) and the credit
   memo chased to receipt.

==================================================================
6. OPEN LINEAR / AIRTABLE ITEMS TOUCHING NORTHSTAR
==================================================================
Linear (open, tagged northstar_legal / crownpeak):
  - "Append VEN-030-736427 stale-void write-up…" — in_progress
  - "Document CrownPeak IR coding-helper rule…" — in_progress
  - "Attach CrownPeak coding-classification clarification…" — in_progress
  - "Review CrownPeak AP queue / tune duplicate detection" — todo
  - "Add partner sign-off conditions to CrownPeak duplicate-detection
    follow-up" — todo
  - "Close out CrownPeak three-item follow-up arc" — marked done, but the
    underlying SAP invoices are still open (see §5), so the arc is not
    operationally complete.
Airtable: no existing Northstar engagement record found in the AP
  Workflow Exceptions, Close Blocker Triage, Tax Cadence, Annual Report
  or Onboarding tables (the Northstar AP backlog was not being tracked
  there). A record has now been created to reflect engagement status.

==================================================================
7. DOES THE STORY MATCH THE NUMBERS?
==================================================================
  - AP CrownPeak dispositions: MISMATCH — comms say resolved/approved,
    SAP shows still pending. Execution gap + outstanding credit memo.
  - FY2025 annual accounts / tax close: MATCH — partner-draw reclass
    ($18,750 → 211000) and SALT late-post ($4,820.30) tie to the TB and
    have documented sign-offs (Andrea, William).
  - WIP: CONSISTENT but PROVISIONAL — comms said a recognition JE was
    held pending hours tie-out; GL shows the May cycle mid-close. Not a
    clean tie-out yet.
  - Scope: GAP — comms/work reflect broad accounting/tax/advisory
    delivery; only an FY2026 audit letter is on file.

==================================================================
8. RECOMMENDATIONS FOR THE PARTNER MEETING
==================================================================
  1. SCOPE: confirm whether a current accounting/tax/advisory engagement
     letter exists for Northstar. If not, quantify the recurring
     accounting + multi-state tax + advisory effort (baseline vs actual
     by step) and decide absorb vs documented change order. Address the
     audit-independence/self-review exposure explicitly.
  2. AP BACKLOG: prioritise clearing the 87-item / $302K queue; execute
     the partner-approved CrownPeak dispositions in SAP and chase the
     CrownPeak credit memo to receipt. Treat the >180-day tail (50 inv /
     $177K) as a control finding, not BAU.
  3. WIP: finish the May recognition cycle and tie WIP to confirmed hours
     before any client/partner reporting.
  4. AR: raise Northstar's client-AR collectibility (allowance > gross
     AR; $427K YTD bad debt) with the client; feed into FY2026 audit
     planning.

Supporting detail (invoice-level AP listing, JE references, Slack/email
breadcrumbs) available on request / in the linked source systems.

input.subject: Northstar Legal — full engagement status review (AR / WIP / scope / AP) for partner meeting
input.content:
Daniel,

Per your ask this morning, here is the full picture on Northstar Legal across AR, WIP, scope and AP — pulled from the GL, SAP sub-ledger, Records Vault, Linear, Airtable and the Slack/email record. Full working paper is filed in Records Vault: doc_b0032cd2392c ("Northstar Legal — Engagement Status Review", restricted, AICPA_SQMS_7Y). I've also opened an engagement-status record in Airtable (AP Workflow Exceptions). Headlines below; enough to walk into the Matthew meeting without surprises.

THE TWO THINGS THAT MATTER MOST
1) Scope/engagement-letter gap. The ONLY Northstar engagement letter on file is the FY2026 AUDIT letter (executed 2026-05-11). There is NO accounting/bookkeeping/tax/advisory engagement letter in the vault — yet we are clearly doing all of it: recurring monthly close/bookkeeping, FY2025 annual accounts, FY2025 federal 1065 + multi-state/SALT tax, quarterly partner packages, FP-2026-04 management accounts, AP processing, and partner-level tax-planning advisory (~$180K projected savings model). Either a separate accounting/tax EL exists outside the vault (needs confirming) or this recurring work is undocumented. The advisory modeling and the multi-state SALT clean-up are the clearest out-of-scope / over-baseline candidates — same pattern I flagged on Acme in May (MMA wrap ~15 hrs over the EL baseline on TX/GA/NC sales-tax coding), and there's precedent: the prior Northstar engagement had a documented AR-aging-cleanup hourly cap we reused as the Acme benchmark. Also a self-review/independence flag: we're set to audit FY2026 while keeping the books and doing the tax return for the same client.
   → Recommendation: confirm whether a current accounting/tax/advisory EL exists. If not, we quantify the recurring + advisory effort (baseline vs actual by step, like Acme) so you can decide absorb vs documented change order with Matthew.

2) AP backlog on the Northstar book. 87 vendor invoices sitting in pending_approval = $302,063.22 as of 2026-06-12. 75 invoices ($274,917) are >90 days; 50 ($177,402) are >180 days; oldest is 336 days (CrownPeak VEN-030-817856, $1,152.41). Split 210000 $220,855 / 219000 $81,208. This is structural, not a few stuck items, and it overlaps the FY2026 audit period.

COMMS vs NUMBERS — does the story match?
- CrownPeak: MISMATCH. The dispositions were discussed and partner-approved (you/Matthew) in Slack/email/Linear — void VEN-030-736427 as stale, recode VEN-030-817856 210000→219000 and release, dismiss VEN-030-353041 as a duplicate — but ALL THREE are still pending_approval in SAP. The narrative says resolved; the system says open. The CrownPeak credit memo promised "within 5 business days" is also not yet evidenced in file. These need to be executed (void/recode/release) and the credit memo chased.
- FY2025 annual accounts / tax: MATCH. The partner-draw reclass ($18,750 → 211000) and the SALT late-post ($4,820.30) tie to the Northstar TB and have documented sign-offs (Andrea, William).
- WIP: CONSISTENT but PROVISIONAL. A Northstar WIP-recognition JE was held pending the billable-hours tie-out / client input; the May cycle is mid-close (entries still draft/submitted), so WIP does not present a clean tie-out yet. Northstar-book WIP = $2,013,943.
- Scope: GAP (see #1).

AR — outstanding & staleness
Note: Brookfield's GL does not tag firm-level AR/WIP by client, so a precise Brookfield-side "fees receivable from Northstar" aging can't be cut from the GL alone (firm-wide AR $1.25M, WIP $2.14M with no May movement) — recommend confirming client-level fee AR/WIP from the billing system. On the Northstar book we keep: AR $647,359; Allowance for Doubtful Accounts $1,057,728 (larger than gross AR); WIP $2,013,943; Deferred Revenue $2,043,052; YTD bad-debt expense $427,019. That allowance/bad-debt profile says a meaningful slice of Northstar's client receivables is already aged/doubtful and at risk — worth raising with the client and feeding into FY2026 audit collectibility testing.

Open Linear/Airtable
Linear: several CrownPeak / northstar_legal follow-ups still open (in_progress: stale-void write-up, IR coding-helper rule, coding-classification; todo: AP-queue/duplicate-detection review, partner sign-off conditions). The "close out CrownPeak three-item arc" issue is marked done, but the underlying SAP invoices are still open — so it isn't operationally complete. Airtable: no pre-existing Northstar engagement record; I've created one.

Recommendations for the partner meeting
1. Scope — confirm EL coverage; if none for accounting/tax/advisory, quantify effort and decide absorb vs change order; address independence.
2. AP — clear the $302K queue; execute the approved CrownPeak dispositions in SAP; chase the credit memo; treat the >180-day tail as a control finding.
3. WIP — finish the May recognition cycle and tie to confirmed hours before reporting.
4. AR — raise Northstar client-AR collectibility; feed into audit planning.

Full detail and invoice-level support in the filed memo. Happy to pull the baseline-vs-actual hours breakdown next if you want it before you see Matthew.

George

content.text:
Done. Here's where the Northstar engagement actually stands, and what I've put in place for Daniel.

## What I found

**1. Scope vs engagement letter — the biggest issue.** The *only* Northstar engagement letter on file in Records Vault is the **FY2026 AUDIT letter (executed 2026‑05‑11)**. There is **no accounting/bookkeeping/tax/advisory engagement letter** on file — yet we're demonstrably doing all of it: recurring monthly close, FY2025 annual accounts, FY2025 federal 1065 + multi‑state/SALT tax, quarterly packages, FP‑2026‑04 management accounts, AP processing, and partner‑level tax‑planning advisory (~$180K projected savings model). So either a separate accounting/tax EL exists outside the vault, or this recurring + advisory work is **undocumented / out of scope** — a change‑order candidate. It mirrors the Acme MMA "15 hrs over EL baseline" pattern you flagged in May, and there's Northstar precedent (the AR‑aging cap reused on Acme). Plus a **self‑review/independence flag**: auditing FY2026 while keeping the books and preparing the return.

**2. AP backlog (the "queue" rumor is real).** 87 Northstar vendor invoices in `pending_approval` = **$302,063** as of 6/12. **75 inv / $274,917 >90 days; 50 inv / $177,402 >180 days; oldest 336 days** (CrownPeak VEN‑030‑817856). Structural, and it overlaps the audit period.

**3. Comms vs numbers — one clear mismatch.** The CrownPeak dispositions (void / recode / duplicate‑dismiss) were **partner‑approved in Slack/email/Linear but never executed in SAP** — all three still pending; promised credit memo not yet in file. By contrast the FY2025 close ties out (partner‑draw reclass $18,750→211000 and SALT $4,820.30 both match the TB with sign‑offs).

**4. WIP / AR.** WIP is **provisional** — a Northstar WIP‑recognition JE was held pending the hours tie‑out and the May cycle is mid‑close, so it doesn't yet tie cleanly. The GL doesn't tag firm AR/WIP by client (flagged honestly), but on Northstar's own book the allowance ($1.06M) exceeds gross AR ($647K) with $427K YTD bad debt — material staleness/collectibility risk.

**5. Open items.** Multiple CrownPeak/northstar Linear issues still in_progress/todo (and the "close‑out arc" is marked done while the SAP invoices are still open). No pre‑existing Northstar engagement record in Airtable.

## What I did
- **Filed the working paper** to Records Vault → `doc_b0032cd2392c` (restricted, AICPA_SQMS_7Y, retention to 2033).
- **Created an Airtable engagement‑status record** (AP Workflow Exceptions, `recfc8d0e419d964f`, High/Open) capturing all four findings.
- **Emailed you and Daniel** the full briefing (`b2a7e9e22d0246f1`) with the figures and recommendations.

One judgment call worth flagging: I couldn't read the audit EL's actual scope text (the vault doc is a stub), so the scope comparison is title‑based — I noted that in the memo and recommended confirming whether a separate accounting/tax EL exists before treating the work as definitively out of scope. If you want, I can pull the baseline‑vs‑actual hours breakdown next so Daniel has the change‑order number ready for Matthew.
